patterns.label: ADO Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Highest-frequency email signal this cycle (20 instances/week). Machine-generated ADO notifications are structurally predictable — typed by work-item category (bug, epic, requirement, task). A routing skill can filter by relevance tier, surface only actionable items, and batch the rest into a daily digest. Saves ~30–45 min of cognitive triage load per week.

patterns.label: Eval Strategy & Leadership Thread Engagement
patterns.llmRationale: High-signal email threads spanning eval strategy, governance, and cross-org coordination (EvalCon, Project Crucible, customer eval playbook). Multi-party, multi-reply chains with director-level stakeholders. Automation ceiling limited by judgment requirements, but a synthesizer skill extracting decisions, surfacing open questions, and drafting aligned replies would preserve strategic focus while eliminating re-read overhead.

patterns.label: SEVAL Incident Announcement Monitoring
patterns.llmRationale: Clustered lifecycle email notifications (alert to mitigation to resolution). Well-structured subject lines and predictable phase vocabulary. A skill can parse incident lifecycle phase, classify required action (monitor-only vs respond), and surface only unresolved items requiring human attention.

patterns.label: Internal Events, Training & Compliance Alert Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Consistent weekly volume (~9 items) of mixed-category emails: all-hands reminders, training invites, compliance file-blocked alerts, GitHub admin notices. Low decision density per item but meaningful aggregate triage time. Automatable by tagging each as attend/skip/action-required/FYI with a one-line rationale.

patterns.label: Weekly Recurring Internal Meeting Rhythm
patterns.llmRationale: Five distinct weekly internal meetings (pod standup, Project Crucible v-team, CAPE customer sync, CAPECAT-PM review, architecture call) consuming ~3.25h/week. Consistent attendees and recurring agenda types make automated prep briefs and post-meeting notes high-value. Acts as HEAD of downstream document and email chain.

patterns.label: Learning & Scaling Fridays Deep-Work Session
patterns.llmRationale: High-duration weekly deep-work and reflection session (~2h55m). Core content is creative and collaborative — low automation ceiling for the session itself. However, pre-session agenda scaffolding, reflection prompt generation, and post-session synthesis of learnings are automatable sub-components with meaningful time savings.

patterns.label: SEVAL / BizChat Office Hours (Multi-Region, High Scale)
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly multi-region open office hours with hundreds of field and engineering attendees. Extreme scale multiplier — routing even 50% of questions to a FAQ skill eliminates hours of live Q&A bottleneck per week. Recurring question clusters are highly predictable (eval thresholds, known issues, lifecycle guidance). Highest participant-weighted value pattern in this dataset.

patterns.label: External Customer-Facing Meetings (Weekly Pattern)
patterns.llmRationale: Highest absolute time commitment this cycle (7.75h/wk across 8 engagements with major partners). Each meeting follows a consistent prep pattern — customer context, eval status, agenda, talking points — requiring per-customer tailoring. A prep-pack skill parameterized by customer name and engagement type would recover ~4h/wk of prep time.

patterns.label: Recurring Evals Strategy Q&A (Expert Bottleneck)
patterns.llmRationale: 10 Teams instances this week of teammates seeking eval strategy interpretation — PM is the de facto source of truth for eval philosophy, target-setting, lifecycle framing, and customer positioning. Classic BSI (Bottleneck Saturation Index) pattern. A skill encoding this knowledge provides 24/7 self-service access, reduces PM interrupt cost, and scales domain expertise across the entire field organization.

patterns.label: Eval Product Judgment & Triage (Bug vs Limitation vs Design)
patterns.llmRationale: 7 Teams escalations asking PM to classify eval behaviors: bug, limitation, or expected design. Also covers preview vs GA status and inconsistent results. Questions follow a decision-tree structure amenable to a classification skill. Estimated 60–70% case resolution rate for a triage guide, escalating only genuinely novel edge cases.

patterns.label: Customer Eval Escalation & Unblocking (De Facto DRI)
patterns.llmRationale: PM repeatedly pulled in as de-facto DRI when customer eval engagements hit blockers: ICM/ADO triage, reproduction guidance, known-issue matching, escalation routing. Automatable components include ICM/ADO status lookup and known-issue classification; judgment-heavy components must still escalate. High value: directly unblocks customer revenue paths.

patterns.label: Field Enablement Deck & FAQ Authoring Requests
patterns.llmRationale: Repeated Teams requests for audience-tailored eval enablement materials: decks, FAQs, talking points, customer-messaging validation. Output structure is known and consistent; variation is by audience depth. A skill generating enablement packs from a canonical content source with an audience parameter would eliminate per-request bespoke authoring.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Sharing (Canonical Link Hub)
patterns.llmRationale: 8 instances of PM acting as human link registry — sharing canonical locations for GitHub repos, playbook versions, SharePoint files, Loop components, and ZIP archives. A self-maintaining artifact registry with natural-language aliases would eliminate these lookup requests entirely.

patterns.label: Cross-Surface Status Update Reformatting (Loop to Slides to Email)
patterns.llmRationale: Same status content (highlights, blockers, next steps) repeatedly reformatted for different M365 surfaces: Loop pages, PowerPoint slides, email summaries, Teams posts. Underlying content structure is stable; friction is entirely mechanical surface-specific formatting. Very high automation ceiling. Strong multi-source confirmation (Teams requests + document activity).

patterns.label: Weekly Loop Tracker & Status Report Contribution
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly contributions to Loop-based engagement tracker (customer name, agent type, tooling, status) and status reports (highlights/lowlights/milestones). Known schema and cadence. A skill parsing meeting notes and recent email signals into structured tracker entries would eliminate manual copy-paste and reduce update latency.

patterns.label: Eval Template & Guidance Artifact Curation
patterns.llmRationale: Eval templates and guidance artifacts reused at the start of each new customer engagement. Excel eval set templates, enablement decks, internal guidance — retrieved and per-customer-customized on demand. A skill instantiating and pre-filling templates from a customer profile would accelerate engagement ramp time.

patterns.label: Notification to Review Meeting to Loop Doc Sequential Workflow
patterns.llmRationale: Explicit repeating 3-step cross-source workflow: notification/email received, review meeting triggered, Loop doc created or updated. Appears ~3x/week. Classic Chain Head pattern: automating the head (notification routing + meeting prep) cascades ~0.7x savings to downstream steps. Strongest multi-source chain in this dataset with clear entry and exit points at each stage.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Artifact Recreation Across Team Members
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple team members independently create overlapping eval artifacts in different formats: checklists (Word), templates (Excel), scorecards (PowerPoint), decision trees (Figma). Post-hoc merging is observed repeatedly. Philosophy is aligned; duplication is in format and sequencing. A standardizer skill generating canonical artifacts on demand would eliminate parallel creation waste and enforce cross-team consistency.

patterns.label: Customer Feedback Synthesis (Repeated From-Scratch Pattern)
patterns.llmRationale: Repeated explicit asks to read long feedback documents, summarize into themes, list impacted customers, and tie to actions. Output format is known and consistent. Cognitively expensive but structurally predictable — textbook automation candidate. High strategic value: feeds directly into product decisions and VoC programs.

patterns.label: AI Skill Builder Tooling Iteration (Meta-Pattern)
patterns.llmRationale: Iterative development of the skill-detector system itself: ZIP archives, shell scripts, JSON configs, SKILL.md specs, HTML dashboards. This meta-pattern (building AI skills to automate work) consumes ~3.25h/wk and is a growing area of activity. A scaffold skill could accelerate SKILL.md authoring, iteration cycles, and dashboard refresh — compounding value across all other skill-building work.

candidateSkills.name: external-meeting-prep-pack
candidateSkills.description: Generates tailored pre-meeting briefs for external customer and partner engagements. Given a customer name and meeting type, produces: customer context summary, current eval engagement status, agenda scaffold, suggested talking points, and key questions to ask.
triggerExamples: External meeting with a major partner on the calendar for tomorrow
Need to prep for the weekly customer engagement sync
Partner call coming up — need context on their current eval engagement
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~30–40 min of bespoke prep per external meeting. At 8 meetings/week, recovers ~4–5h/week of strategic thinking time while improving consistency of customer-facing messaging.

candidateSkills.name: eval-artifact-standardizer
candidateSkills.description: Generates canonical eval artifacts (checklists, scorecards, templates, decision trees) on demand from a shared content model. Prevents parallel independent creation by multiple team members and enforces cross-team consistency across formats.
triggerExamples: Need an eval checklist for a new agent deployment
Starting a new customer engagement and need the eval set template
Multiple people on the team are making overlapping scorecards
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates duplication where multiple team members independently create the same artifacts in different formats. Recovers ~1h/week and ensures philosophical alignment across all customer-facing materials.

candidateSkills.name: office-hours-faq-router
candidateSkills.description: Routes and pre-answers recurring questions from large-scale office hours. Maintains a living FAQ from past sessions, classifies incoming questions by type, and generates confident answers for known question clusters.
triggerExamples: Preparing for weekly multi-region office hours
Field engineer asking about eval threshold recommendations
Same question came up in three separate office hours sessions
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Scale multiplier: deflecting 50% of questions with 200+ attendees eliminates hours of live PM bottleneck per week and provides 24/7 self-service access to eval guidance for the entire field organization.

candidateSkills.name: customer-feedback-synthesizer
candidateSkills.description: Reads long customer feedback documents or comment threads and synthesizes into: top themes, list of impacted customers per theme, suggested product or process actions, and a concise executive summary — using a consistent, known output format.
triggerExamples: Lots of customer feedback arrived from the eval rollout — need synthesis
Can you read these comments and pull out the themes and impacted customers?
VoC review is tomorrow — need themes and actions by EOD
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates cognitively expensive but structurally predictable synthesis work (~50 min per session). Delivers consistent, decision-ready VoC packages that directly feed product prioritization and leadership reviews.

candidateSkills.name: cross-surface-status-reformatter
candidateSkills.description: Takes a status update in one M365 surface format and reformats it for another target surface. Preserves highlights, blockers, and next-steps structure while applying surface-appropriate formatting and length norms.
triggerExamples: Convert this Loop status page into executive slides for the leadership review
Turn these meeting notes into the weekly email update
Reformat the highlights and lowlights into a Teams channel post
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~1h/week of mechanical reformatting work. The content already exists — friction is purely surface-specific adaptation, which is 100% automatable once the source structure is stable.

candidateSkills.name: eval-strategy-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring eval strategy questions with PM-grade depth. Covers setting realistic eval targets, fitting evals into agent development lifecycle, explaining eval-driven development to customers, and interpreting eval metrics — based on canonical organizational eval philosophy.
triggerExamples: How should I set eval thresholds for this customer's agent?
How have you been framing eval-driven development with customers?
Does this eval score mean the agent is ready for production?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Provides 24/7 self-service access to PM-level eval expertise, eliminating a high-frequency bottleneck (10+ questions/week). Scales domain knowledge across the entire field organization without requiring PM interrupt.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Triages ADO work-item notification emails by type and relevance tier. Routes high-priority items requiring immediate attention, batches low-priority notifications into a daily digest, and surfaces action-required items with one-line summaries.
triggerExamples: 20+ ADO notifications have arrived since yesterday morning
Morning email triage — ADO eval work-item backlog
Which of these eval work items actually need my attention today?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Highest-frequency email triage pattern (20 items/week). Eliminates ~35–40 min/week of repetitive notification scanning, replacing it with a 2-min prioritized digest review.

candidateSkills.name: field-enablement-pack-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates audience-tailored enablement materials for field teams: slide summaries, FAQ documents, talking points, and customer-messaging guidance. Parameterized by audience type (executive, technical, field PM, customer success) and topic area.
triggerExamples: Need a deck summarizing evals for a non-technical customer audience
Put together FAQ talking points for the field team about eval thresholds
Regional PM needs enablement materials for an upcoming customer workshop
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates per-request bespoke authoring for a recurring request pattern (4+ times/week). A canonical content source with an audience parameter produces consistent, validated messaging at scale across all field regions.
